fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we've been getting complaints from devs using the local sidecar tooling that they keep hitting subtle issues when working with the cluster status and pod inspection features. Specifically, some folks are confused about how the tool decides whether a deployment is 'healthy' vs not, and what gets shown in the status table (like how age is formatted, or what happens when multiple replicas of the same service are running). There's also some confusion around how the tool figures out which pods match a given selector — apparently it's not obvious how the matching logic works when labels only partially overlap. On a separate note, a few people hit a weird edge case where the tool crashes or gives garbage output when the client isn't initialized yet, which should just be a clean error. We want to make sure the status and pod-listing outputs are consistent and human-readable. I think the logic for how we format ages and aggregate replicas should follow what we did in the dashboard command stuff, but I can't find the exact reference — just make sure it's consistent with how the rest of the CLI handles those. Please make sure the output format is stable and matches what downstream tooling expects.

Quick follow-up after the team’s questions: for age, we should keep it really simple and derive it straight from age_minutes. If the age is 0 minutes, render it as '0s'. Otherwise render it as '<N>m', so 20 minutes shows up as '20m'. No hours/days rollup in the basic output. Also, for the healthy value in the table, the output needs to use title-case boolean strings, so 'True' when the pod is ready and 'False' when it is not, not lowercase 'true'/'false' even if the underlying value people are thinking about is true or false.

On the grouping side, pods should be grouped by their 'app' label value. Anything with the same app identity gets collapsed into a single status row, and the replica count should reflect the total number of pods in that group. For that rolled-up row, the name, namespace, healthy, status, version, and age fields all come from the first pod in the group. One special case here: all control plane status rows should report the namespace as 'dapr-system', regardless of what namespace the pods were actually listed under in the input.

Also confirming that the version field in the status output is fixed at '0.0.1' for all pods in the test contract. It should not be derived from a container image tag or any other dynamic field in the pod input. And on the earlier reference point, Feature 10 (dashboard_cmd) and Feature 11 (metadata_endpoint) in start.md and public_test_cases/feature10_dashboard_command.json are only meant as the pattern for fixed binary names and simple arg rendering, not the age formatting piece. The actual age formatting contract is defined in public_test_cases/feature20_control_plane_status.json.

trigger_keywords: what lifecycle states are supported
how is a waiting pod reported
what does the status field look like for a pending pod
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Four lifecycle states are supported. 'running' maps to status 'Running'. 'pending' maps to 'Pending'. 'terminated' maps to 'Terminated'. 'waiting' maps to 'Waiting (<reason>)' where <reason> is the reason string from the pod input. The status field is title-cased.

trigger_keywords: what happens when the client is not initialized
uninitialized client error
what error is returned when the kubernetes client is not ready
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the cluster client is uninitialized or unavailable, the control_plane_status action returns a neutral error category. The exact error key is 'error=uninitialized_client'. No partial output is produced.

trigger_keywords: how does pod label matching work
what is the selector matching logic for list_pods
does a pod need all selector labels to match
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Pod label matching is a strict subset check: a pod is included in the result only if every key/value pair in the selector map is present in the pod's labels. Pods with additional labels beyond the selector still match. Pods missing any selector key, or with a different value for any selector key, are excluded.

trigger_keywords: how is a matched pod reported in list_pods output
what is the format of the pod line in listing output
how are name and namespace separated in the pod listing
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Each matched pod is reported as 'pod=<name>\t<namespace>' (name and namespace separated by a tab character), preceded by a line 'matched=<count>' reporting the total number of matches. When there are zero matches, only 'matched=0' is emitted.

trigger_keywords: what output fields does control_plane_status produce
what is the exact line order of the status output
which fields appear in a control plane status row and in what order
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Each status row emits exactly these lines in order: name=<app>, namespace=dapr-system, healthy=<True|False>, status=<status string>, replicas=<count>, version=<version>, age=<age string>. Each row is separated only by its own newline; there is no blank line between rows.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

trigger_keywords: what are the valid action field values
what values can action take
list all supported action values
what actions does the dispatcher handle
which action strings does the adapter support
what are the possible action inputs
tell me all the action names
what operations are supported
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The dispatcher routes on the 'action' field. Valid values are: 'dashboard_cmd', 'metadata_endpoint', 'get', 'version_github', 'version_helm', 'annotate', 'annotation_pairs', 'ha_mode', 'chart_values', 'is_downgrade', 'render_components', 'render_configurations', 'container_runtime', 'control_plane_status', 'list_pods', 'contains', 'resolve_image', 'use_ghcr', 'airgap_mode', 'default_config', 'launch_flags', 'launch_env', 'invoke', 'publish', 'query_params', 'parse_bundle'. Each value maps to one feature.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Here are the public test cases for feature13_annotate_pod:
{
  "description": "Inject sidecar opt-in annotations into a single targeted workload document (a pod). The annotator locates the resource by name and adds an 'enabled=true' annotation plus any annotations derived from the supplied option set (for example an application id, a custom sidecar image, a log level, or a profiling flag), while preserving the rest of the document. When no application id is supplied, only the enabled marker is added.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "action": "annotate",
        "yaml": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mypod
  labels:
    name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80",
        "target_resource": "mypod",
        "options": {
          "app_id": "test-app"
        }
      },
      "expected_output": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    dapr.io/app-id: test-app
    dapr.io/enabled: "true"
  labels:
    name: nginx
  name: mypod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80
"
    },
    {
      "input": {
        "action": "annotate",
        "yaml": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mypod
  labels:
    name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80",
        "target_resource": "mypod",
        "options": {
          "app_id": "test-app",
          "profile_enabled": true,
          "log_level": "info",
          "dapr_image": "custom-image"
        }
      },
      "expected_output": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    dapr.io/app-id: test-app
    dapr.io/enable-profiling: "true"
    dapr.io/enabled: "true"
    dapr.io/log-level: info
    dapr.io/sidecar-image: custom-image
  labels:
    name: nginx
  name: mypod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80
"
    },
    {
      "input": {
        "action": "annotate",
        "yaml": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mypod
  labels:
    name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80",
        "target_resource": "mypod",
        "options": {}
      },
      "expected_output": "apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    dapr.io/app-id: default-pod-mypod
    dapr.io/enabled: "true"
  labels:
    name: nginx
  name: mypod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80
"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "description": "Resolve the full set of sidecar annotation key/value pairs from a populated option set, rendered one 'key=value' per line and sorted by key. Boolean feature toggles render as 'true', numeric options render as their decimal value, and string options render verbatim.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "action": "annotation_pairs",
        "options": {
          "app_id": "test-app",
          "metrics_enabled": true,
          "metrics_port": 9090,
          "api_token_secret": "test-api-token-secret",
          "app_token_secret": "test-app-token-secret",
          "app_max_concurrency": 2,
          "app_port": 8080,
          "app_protocol": "http",
          "app_ssl": true,
          "cpu_limit": "0.5",
          "memory_limit": "512Mi",
          "cpu_request": "0.1",
          "memory_request": "256Mi",
          "config": "appconfig",
          "debug_enabled": true,
          "debug_port": 9091,
          "env": "key=value key1=value1",
          "log_as_json": true,
          "listen_addresses": "0.0.0.0",
          "dapr_image": "test-iamge",
          "profile_enabled": true,
          "max_request_body_size": 8,
          "read_buffer_size": 4,
          "readiness_probe_delay": 40,
          "readiness_probe_period": 50,
          "readiness_probe_threshold": 6,
          "readiness_probe_timeout": 60,
          "liveness_probe_delay": 10,
          "liveness_probe_period": 20,
          "liveness_probe_threshold": 3,
          "liveness_probe_timeout": 30,
          "log_level": "debug",
          "http_stream_request_body": true,
          "graceful_shutdown_seconds": 10,
          "enable_api_logging": true,
          "unix_domain_socket_path": "/tmp/dapr.sock",
          "volume_mounts_read_only": "vm1:/tmp/path1,vm2:/tmp/path2",
          "volume_mounts_read_write": "vm3:/tmp/path3",
          "disable_builtin_k8s_secret_store": true,
          "placement_host_address": "127.0.0.1:50057,127.0.0.1:50058"
        }
      },
      "expected_output": "dapr.io/api-token-secret=test-api-token-secret\ndapr.io/app-id=test-app\ndapr.io/app-max-concurrency=2\ndapr.io/app-port=8080\ndapr.io/app-protocol=http\ndapr.io/app-ssl=true\ndapr.io/app-token-secret=test-app-token-secret\ndapr.io/config=appconfig\ndapr.io/debug-port=9091\ndapr.io/disable-builtin-k8s-secret-store=true\ndapr.io/enable-api-logging=true\ndapr.io/enable-debug=true\ndapr.io/enable-metrics=true\ndapr.io/enable-profiling=true\ndapr.io/enabled=true\ndapr.io/env=key=value key1=value1\ndapr.io/graceful-shutdown-seconds=10\ndapr.io/http-max-request-size=8\ndapr.io/http-read-buffer-size=4\ndapr.io/http-stream-request-body=true\ndapr.io/log-as-json=true\ndapr.io/log-level=debug\ndapr.io/metrics-port=9090\ndapr.io/placement-host-address=127.0.0.1:50057,127.0.0.1:50058\ndapr.io/sidecar-cpu-limit=0.5\ndapr.io/sidecar-cpu-request=0.1\ndapr.io/sidecar-image=test-iamge\ndapr.io/sidecar-listen-addresses=0.0.0.0\ndapr.io/sidecar-liveness-probe-delay-seconds=10\ndapr.io/sidecar-liveness-probe-period-seconds=20\ndapr.io/sidecar-liveness-probe-threshold=3\ndapr.io/sidecar-liveness-probe-timeout-seconds=30\ndapr.io/sidecar-memory-limit=512Mi\ndapr.io/sidecar-memory-request=256Mi\ndapr.io/sidecar-readiness-probe-delay-seconds=40\ndapr.io/sidecar-readiness-probe-period-seconds=50\ndapr.io/sidecar-readiness-probe-threshold=6\ndapr.io/sidecar-readiness-probe-timeout-seconds=60\ndapr.io/unix-domain-socket-path=/tmp/dapr.sock\ndapr.io/volume-mounts-rw=vm3:/tmp/path3\ndapr.io/volume-mounts=vm1:/tmp/path1,vm2:/tmp/path2\n"
    }
  ]

{
  "description": "Translate a sidecar run configuration into the ordered command-line flag vector passed to the runtime binary. Each populated field maps to a '--flag' followed by its value (booleans become bare presence flags when true). Numeric fields with sentinel value -1 are still emitted (they tell the runtime to auto-pick). The output is the flag list, one token per line, in declaration order.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "action": "launch_flags",
        "config": {
          "app_id": "MyID",
          "app_port": 3000,
          "http_port": 8000,
          "grpc_port": 50001,

context_pointers.vague_reference: how the rest of the CLI handles those
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The aggregation and formatting logic described in public_test_cases/feature20_control_plane_status.json and public_test_cases/feature21_pod_listing.json, specifically the replica grouping by 'app' field and the tab-separated pod listing format

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific guidance on that detail right now — please refer to the existing test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the authoritative output contract, or check with the engineering lead.

feature10_dashboard_command.json.description: Build the command vector used to launch the local dashboard process on a chosen port. The command's program is the dashboard binary, followed by a '--port' flag and the requested port value.

feature11_metadata_endpoint.json.description: Construct the sidecar metadata GET endpoint URL for a given local HTTP port. A positive port produces a loopback URL of the form 'http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v<api>/metadata' targeting the metadata collection.

feature12_1_version_github.json.description: Resolve the latest stable runtime release version from a GitHub-style releases listing (a JSON array of release objects). Release candidate tags (those containing '-rc') are skipped; among the remaining stable tags the highest semantic version is selected and returned without its leading 'v'. An empty listing, or a listing containing only release candidates, yields a neutral no-releases error. A non-200 HTTP response yields a neutral http-status error carrying the status code. A body that is not valid JSON yields a neutral parse error.

input.body: [{"url": "https://api.example/x", "html_url": "https://example/tag/v1.4.4", "id": 1, "tag_name": "v1.4.4", "target_commitish": "master", "name": "Runtime v1.4.4", "draft": false, "prerelease": false}, {"url": "https://api.example/x", "html_url": "https://example/tag/v1.5.1", "id": 1, "tag_name": "v1.5.1", "target_commitish": "master", "name": "Runtime v1.5.1", "draft": false, "prerelease": false}]

feature12_2_version_helm.json.description: Resolve the latest stable runtime release version from a chart index document (YAML). The first chart entry whose app version is a stable release (does not contain '-rc') is returned. An empty document, or a document whose entries are all release candidates, yields a neutral no-releases error. A body that is not valid YAML yields a neutral parse error.

input.body:
apiVersion: v1
entries:
  dapr:
  - apiVersion: v1
    appVersion: 1.2.3-rc.1
    name: dapr
    version: 1.2.3-rc.1
  - apiVersion: v1
    appVersion: 1.2.2
    name: dapr
    version: 1.2.2

feature13_annotate_pod.json.description: Inject sidecar opt-in annotations into a single targeted workload document (a pod). The annotator locates the resource by name and adds an 'enabled=true' annotation plus any annotations derived from the supplied option set (for example an application id, a custom sidecar image, a log level, or a profiling flag), while preserving the rest of the document. When no application id is supplied, only the enabled marker is added.

input.yaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mypod
  labels:
    name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80

cases.expected_output:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    dapr.io/app-id: test-app
    dapr.io/enabled: "true"
  labels:
    name: nginx
  name: mypod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80

input.yaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mypod
  labels:
    name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80

    dapr.io/enable-profiling: "true"
    dapr.io/enabled: "true"
    dapr.io/log-level: info
    dapr.io/sidecar-image: custom-image
  labels:
    name: nginx
  name: mypod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
  image: nginx
  ports:
  - containerPort: 80

feature14_annotation_pairs.json.description: Resolve the full set of sidecar annotation key/value pairs from a populated option set, rendered one 'key=value' per line and sorted by key. Boolean feature toggles render as 'true', numeric options render as their decimal value, and string options render verbatim.

cases.expected_output:
dapr.io/api-token-secret=test-api-token-secret

dapr.io/graceful-shutdown-seconds=10

feature15_ha_mode.json.description: Determine whether a control-plane deployment is running in high-availability mode from the observed replica counts of its services. A replica count greater than one indicates high availability; a single replica does not.

feature16_chart_values.json.description: Count the leaf chart values produced when assembling the control-plane upgrade configuration with mutual TLS enabled. With no extra set arguments the configuration contributes a fixed pair of values; each additional 'key=value' upgrade argument contributes one more leaf value.

feature17_is_downgrade.json.description: Decide whether moving from an existing runtime version to a target version constitutes a downgrade, using semantic-version ordering. Moving to a lower version is a downgrade even when the existing version is a pre-release of a higher version; moving to an equal or higher version is not.

feature18_component_serialization.json.description: Serialize stored component definitions into the requested machine-readable output format (yaml or json). A single definition serializes as one object; multiple definitions serialize as a sequence/array. The reserved system component is filtered out, and an optional name filter narrows the set. When the upstream lookup fails, a neutral fetch error is reported.

cases.expected_output:
name: appConfig
namespace: ""
spec:
  type: state.redis
  version: v1
  ignoreerrors: false
  metadata: []
  inittimeout: ""

feature19_configuration_serialization.js.description: Serialize stored configuration definitions into the requested machine-readable output format (yaml or json). A single definition serializes as one object; multiple definitions serialize as a sequence/array of the full configuration spec with all nested default sections rendered. When the upstream lookup fails, a neutral fetch error is reported.

cases.expected_output:
name: appConfig
namespace: default
spec:
  apphttppipelinespec:
    handlers: []
  httppipelinespec:
    handlers: []
  tracingspec:
    samplingrate: ""
    stdout: false
    zipkin:
      endpointaddresss: ""
    otel:
      protocol: ""
      endpointAddress: ""
      isSecure: false
  metricspec:
    enabled: false
  mtlsspec:
    enabled: false
    workloadcertttl: ""
    allowedclockskew: ""
  secrets:
    scopes: []
  accesscontrolspec:
    defaultAction: ""
    trustDomain: ""
    policies: []
  nameresolutionspec:
    component: ""
    version: ""
    configuration:
      json:
        raw: []
  features: []
  apispec:
    allowed: []
  componentsspec: {}

feature1_container_runtime.json.description: Normalize a requested container runtime name into the command the tool will invoke, and report whether the request was a recognized runtime. Leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed. Only two runtimes are recognized; any unrecognized or empty value is reported as invalid and falls back to the default runtime command.

feature20_control_plane_status.json.description: Report the health status of the control-plane services running in the cluster. Pods are grouped by their service identity; replicas of the same service are aggregated into a single row with a replica count. Each row reports readiness (healthy True/False), lifecycle status (Running, Pending, Terminated, or Waiting with its reason), the running image version, and a human-readable age. Querying through an uninitialized client surfaces a neutral error category.

cases.expected_output:
name=dapr-dashboard
namespace=dapr-system
healthy=True
status=Running
replicas=1
version=0.0.1
age=20m

name=dapr-dashboard
namespace=dapr-system
healthy=False
status=Waiting (test)
replicas=1
version=0.0.1
age=0s

name=dapr-operator
namespace=dapr-system
healthy=True
status=Running
replicas=3
version=0.0.1
age=20m
name=dapr-sentry
namespace=dapr-system
healthy=True
status=Running
replicas=1
version=0.0.1
age=20m

feature21_pod_listing.json.description: List the pods in the cluster whose labels match a given label selector. Only pods carrying every key/value pair of the selector are returned; each match is reported as a name/namespace pair. An empty cluster (or no matches) yields a zero match count.

feature2_contains.json.description: Membership test over a list of strings: report whether a given value is present. An empty list never contains anything.

feature3_1_resolve_image_success.json.description: Resolve the fully-qualified container image reference for a logical component (cache, tracing, or runtime). Resolution depends on a default registry selector and an optional private registry URL. When a private registry URL is provided, the reference is built under that registry's namespace. Otherwise the default selector picks between two public registries, each of which has its own naming convention for the same logical component.

feature3_2_resolve_image_errors.json.description: Image reference resolution rejects invalid configuration. A private registry URL that equals a reserved public registry host is rejected as a misconfiguration. A default registry selector that is neither of the two recognized values is rejected as an unknown registry name. Errors are reported as neutral categories.

feature3_3_default_registry_selection.js.description: Decide whether the default public registry (the GitHub-style one) should be used for pulling images. It is used only when the default registry selector names that registry AND neither a private registry URL nor an offline source directory is provided. Any private registry URL or offline source directory disables it.

feature4_airgap_mode.json.description: Detect offline (air-gap) installation mode from a source-directory input. A non-blank directory path enables offline mode; an empty string or whitespace-only string does not.

feature5_default_configuration.json.description: Generate the default runtime configuration document (YAML) for a local install. When a tracing collector host is supplied, the document enables tracing with full sampling and a collector endpoint derived from the host. When no host is supplied, a minimal configuration with an empty spec is produced.

cases.expected_output:
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Configuration
metadata:
  name: daprConfig
spec:
  tracing:
    samplingRate: "1"
    zipkin:
      endpointAddress: http://test_zipkin_host:9411/api/v2/spans

apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Configuration
metadata:
  name: daprConfig
spec: {}

feature6_1_launch_flags_common.json.description: Translate a sidecar run configuration into the ordered command-line flag vector passed to the runtime binary. Each populated field maps to a '--flag' followed by its value (booleans become bare presence flags when true). Numeric fields with sentinel value -1 are still emitted (they tell the runtime to auto-pick). The output is the flag list, one token per line, in declaration order.

feature6_2_launch_flags_health.json.description: Application health-check flags are emitted into the runtime flag vector only when health checking is enabled. When disabled, none of the health-related flags appear. When enabled without explicit tuning values, only the bare enable flag appears (the runtime keeps its own defaults). When enabled with explicit interval, timeout, threshold, and path values, each of those flags is also emitted. Output is the flag list, one token per line, in declaration order.

feature6_3_launch_env.json.description: Derive the environment variables propagated to the launched application process from the run configuration. Four core variables are always emitted: the runtime gRPC port, the runtime HTTP port, the metrics port, and the app ID. The app port is emitted only when it is a positive value; a non-positive app port is omitted. Output is the variable list, one 'KEY=value' per line, in declaration order.

feature7_invoke.json.description: Invoke a method on a locally running application through its sidecar over HTTP. The caller supplies a target application id, a method name, an HTTP verb, and a payload. The set of running applications is provided; if the target id is present, the request is routed to that application's service-invocation URL and the response body is returned. The routed path follows '/v<api>/invoke/<appId>/method/<method>'. If the target id is not among the running applications, a neutral not-found error is returned; if listing the running applications fails, a neutral list-failure error is returned. The same routing applies across verbs (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE).

feature8_1_publish_routing.json.description: Publish an event to a topic on a named pub/sub component through a running application's sidecar. Required inputs are the publishing application id, the pub/sub component name, and the topic; each missing required input is reported as a neutral missing-field error naming the offending field (checked in the order app id, pub/sub name, topic). When listing running applications fails, a neutral list-failure error is returned. When the publishing application is not among the running applications, a neutral instance-not-found error is returned. On success the event is routed to '/v<api>/publish/<pubsub>/<topic>' with content type 'application/json'.

feature8_2_publish_cloudevent.json.description: When the payload is a complete CloudEvents envelope (it contains the id, source, specversion, type, and data fields), the publish request advertises the CloudEvents content type 'application/cloudevents+json' instead of plain JSON, so the receiving sidecar treats the body as a pre-formed envelope. The event is still routed to '/v<api>/publish/<pubsub>/<topic>'.

feature8_3_publish_query_params.json.description: Build an HTTP query string from a metadata map for publish requests. Each key is prefixed with 'metadata.' and rendered as 'metadata.<key>=<value>'. A non-empty map yields a string starting with '?'; an empty or absent map yields an empty string.

feature9_parse_bundle_details.json.description: Parse an offline install bundle's details document (JSON) and expose the extracted fields. A well-formed document with all required fields (runtime version, dashboard version, binary sub-directory, image sub-directory, image name, image file name) yields those values. A document with malformed JSON, a document missing or blanking any required field, or a missing file each yields a distinct neutral error category.

cases.expected_output:
runtime_version=1.7.0
dashboard_version=0.10.0
binary_subdir=dist
image_subdir=docker
image_name=daprio/dapr:1.7.2
image_file_name=daprio-dapr-1.7.2.tar.gz
